texto: Meggie Folchart: Having writer's block? Maybe I can help.Fenoglio: Oh yes, that's right. You want to be a writer, don't you?Meggie Folchart: You say that as if it's a bad thing.Fenoglio: Oh no, it's just a lonely thing. Sometimes the world you create on the page seems more friendly and alive than the world you actually live in.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a David Lindsay-Abaire: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Meggie Folchart: Having writer's block? Maybe I can help.Fenoglio: Oh yes, that's right. You want…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, writers-block, writing-life pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e writing-life.

texto: From a young age, I was rubbing elbows with a very different kind of person and social class, and I felt a lot of tension and conflict in my identity because of that.
explicacao: Citação de David Lindsay-Abaire sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “From a young age, I was rubbing elbows with a very different kind of person and social class, and…” articula reflexao, conflict, identity em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como identity e young.
